purpose: Define the repo-source line budget as a reasonable-refactor maintenance signal with advisory default behavior and explicit strict ratchet mode for maintainers and scheduled structure jobs.
machine_boundary: This contract owns line-budget thresholds, near-limit advisory thresholds, reasonable-refactor classification, and reviewed oversized-file baselines for tracked source files. It does not define domain truth, production readiness, release readiness, or quality verdict authority.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Family runtime CLI tests share queue, lifecycle, provider, dispatch, approval, and admission fixtures; current line count is reviewed as a strict-ratchet test baseline.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by status/lifecycle, provider/repair, enqueue/approval/hold, attempt ledger, and admission/tick command groups.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Managed update kernel projection tests share update status, plan, apply, projection-only component, and selector fixtures; current line count is reviewed as a strict-ratchet baseline.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by contract/status, plan, projection-only apply guards, companion tools, workflow profile, and selector alias scenarios.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Startup-maintenance tests cover framework archive apply, package-channel artifact apply, rollback, Codex refresh, module sync, OMA install, timeout, and developer checkout scenarios with shared setup.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by framework artifact apply, rollback, Codex refresh, module sync, OMA managed root, health timeout, and developer checkout maintenance scenarios.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Temporal provider tests cover workflow updates, retry, activity state, replay, readiness, closeout, provider blockers, and refs-only history with shared Temporal fixtures.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by contract/readiness, operator updates, retry/state/replay, blocker handling, refs-only history, typed closeout, and rejection scenarios.
